source_authority.interpretation_rule: P2 is normative. P4 supplies serialization and deterministic validation mechanics without changing role ownership, maturity gates, tactic cardinality, evidence burden, append-only state, or external authorization.

json_schema.purpose: Validate presence, exact field set, primitive and nested types, patterns, enums, array cardinality, nested object closure, and locally expressible conditionals.
does_not_validate: Whether evidence supports the associated claim
Whether requested prose stays inside owner scope
Whether maturity and tactic prerequisites are evidenced
Whether the target still reads the declared revision

semantic_validator.purpose: Resolve references and apply authority, provenance, maturity, dependency, concurrency, cross-validation, and stale-revision rules against current project state.
semantic_validator.execution_order: Run only after JSON Schema passes; evaluate every semantic rule in semantic_validations, return all violations in rule order, and use the first code as primary_code without suppressing later codes.

role_authority_matrix.external_authority_rule: authorized_operator is not a canonical analytical role and therefore cannot appear in from_role or to_role. It can appear only as decision owner, gap owner, or escalation target. A handoff never grants its authorization.

semantic_validation_context.reference_resolution: Every opaque reference must resolve inside the same project and at a revision not newer than the handoff. Resolution failure is a semantic error even when the string passes JSON Schema.
semantic_validation_context.side_effect_policy: Validation is read-only. It emits a result and rejection codes; it never rebases, mutates state, authorizes execution, or silently repairs a handoff.

semantic_validations.deterministic_rule: handoff_id is unique in the project; project_ref and cycle_id equal the validation context; from_role and to_role resolve to canonical roles; from_role differs from to_role.
handoff.state_revision must equal both current_state_revision and target_read_revision. No less-than, greater-than, or implicit latest behavior is accepted.

bottleneck_ref resolves to the accepted global bottleneck record for this cycle and revision, or to an explicit unresolved-bottleneck record issued by loop_planning when route status is needs_evidence. Specialist observations never satisfy this check.

Every requested_output.decision_domains value equals a domain owned by to_role; every proposed_state_event is in to_role.allowed_state_events; objective and acceptance criteria cannot instruct acceptance of global bottleneck or an external mutation.
As sets, scope_boundary_next_does_not_decide must equal role_authority_matrix.roles[to_role].required_scope_boundary exactly. Missing and extra domains both fail.

Every decision_ref resolves; owner_role equals decision_domain_owner[decision_domain]; decision state_revision is not newer than handoff.state_revision; its evidence references resolve; requested_output.write_set does not overwrite the accepted decision unless to_role is its canonical owner and an explicit supersession event is requested.

Every required input resolves. A handoff dependency has dependency_status=validated only when present in validated_handoff_registry at the same state_revision; not_applicable requires an explicit route decision. The consumer cannot start otherwise.

Resolve every claim referenced by objective inputs, respected decisions, gaps, and requested output. Each fact has at least one resolved evidence_ref with source_ref and observed_at; every unsupported interpretation is kind=hypothesis and appears in exactly one assumption.claim_refs with rationale and confidence. A claim cannot be both evidenced fact and unsupported assumption.

A non-unknown maturity resolves to an evidenced classification that is at most 30 days old and has no later structural-change event. Otherwise maturity is unknown; mode is minimo_viavel; tactic_refs is empty; escalation includes loop_planning.

Each tactic_ref resolves to one P3 entry; tactic_id, canonical_path, canonical_sha256 and target pillar all match; the entry is selectable; its execution policy permits the requested selection path; no canonical body is copied into the handoff.

Project maturity rank is at least each tactic minimum rank; required prerequisites resolve to evidence or respected decisions; no contraindication matches. Unknown maturity always blocks tactics and falls back to the specialist base method.

Zero tactics means base method. One is default. Two require two distinct requested outputs or a declared dependency, both eligible, no collision, and no semantic duplication. More than two is rejected structurally and semantically.

required=true when multiple roles produce in the cycle, an output constrains another domain, maturity is unknown, a known gap is blocking, a recommendation exceeds current capability, or cycle close is contemplated. Listed roles resolve and conflicts name at least two distinct decision domains. required=false requires both arrays empty.

For a staged parallel batch, every handoff uses the same immutable state_revision; to_role owners and requested_output.write_set values do not overlap; no node depends on a new output from another parallel node; integration is reserved to loop_planning. Any stale member invalidates that member and forces batch revalidation before integration.
If two staged handoffs target the same write_set path, neither wins. Both remain proposals and return to loop_planning for owner-based resolution.

external_execution_authorization is always in the target scope boundary. No requested output, tactic, route, state event, objective, or escalation may be interpreted as operation-specific permission. When external action is needed, a blocking known_gap and authorized_operator escalation are required.
project_ref and all opaque refs contain identifiers only, not secrets, credentials, raw customer corpora, or unnecessary PII. Sensitive content remains behind authorization-aware references.

ERR_HANDOFF_FIELD_MISSING.trigger: JSON Schema failure: missing or extra canonical field, wrong nested shape, invalid type, pattern, enum, or local cardinality.
ERR_HANDOFF_FIELD_MISSING.remedy: Complete the exact 22-field contract and correct structural validation errors.

ERR_HANDOFF_STALE_REVISION.trigger: handoff.state_revision differs from current_state_revision or target_read_revision.
ERR_HANDOFF_STALE_REVISION.remedy: Rehydrate and regenerate or explicitly revalidate the whole handoff against current state.

ERR_HANDOFF_SCOPE_BOUNDARY.trigger: Scope boundary is not the exact complement of the target role's owned decision domains.
ERR_HANDOFF_SCOPE_BOUNDARY.remedy: Populate scope from the canonical owner matrix.

ERR_HANDOFF_OWNER_SCOPE.trigger: Role identity is invalid or objective/requested output/state event asks the target to decide outside its ownership.
ERR_HANDOFF_OWNER_SCOPE.remedy: Split or reroute the objective to the canonical owner.

ERR_HANDOFF_PROVENANCE.trigger: Reference does not resolve, a fact lacks evidence, an unsupported claim lacks assumption metadata, or sensitive content was copied instead of referenced.
ERR_HANDOFF_PROVENANCE.remedy: Attach resolvable provenance, relabel as hypothesis, or minimize the reference.

ERR_SEQUENCE_DEPENDENCY.trigger: A required producer handoff is absent, stale, invalid, or not explicitly not_applicable.
ERR_SEQUENCE_DEPENDENCY.remedy: Complete and validate the dependency before starting the consumer.

ERR_MATURITY_GATE.trigger: Maturity is unsupported or stale, or a tactic exceeds maturity/prerequisites or matches a contraindication.
ERR_MATURITY_GATE.remedy: Use the base method, select a viable alternative, or evidence the missing gate.

ERR_TACTIC_METADATA_MISSING.trigger: A tactic reference lacks a matching complete P3 record or path/hash/owner identity differs.
ERR_TACTIC_METADATA_MISSING.remedy: Use the specialist base method or repair external metadata without editing the prompt.

ERR_TACTIC_CARDINALITY.trigger: More than two tactics are selected or a pair lacks distinct outputs/dependency, eligibility, and noncollision.
ERR_TACTIC_CARDINALITY.remedy: Keep at most two eligible complementary tactics or decompose the route node.

ERR_PARALLELISM_UNSAFE.trigger: Parallel handoffs differ in revision, overlap owner domains, depend on each other's new output, or attempt specialist integration.
ERR_PARALLELISM_UNSAFE.remedy: Serialize or isolate the nodes and revalidate.

ERR_PARALLEL_WRITE_COLLISION.trigger: Two staged parallel outputs share a requested write_set path.
ERR_PARALLEL_WRITE_COLLISION.remedy: Return both proposals to loop_planning; do not choose a winner silently.

ERR_OWNER_SCOPE_VIOLATION.trigger: A requested write would redefine a respected decision owned by another authority.
ERR_OWNER_SCOPE_VIOLATION.remedy: Revert the write and issue a sourced proposal to the owner.

ERR_EXTERNAL_MUTATION_UNAUTHORIZED.trigger: The handoff is used as permission for an external mutation or omits the required authorization gap/escalation.
ERR_EXTERNAL_MUTATION_UNAUTHORIZED.remedy: Remain read-only until operation-specific authorization exists.

ERR_CROSS_VALIDATION_BLOCKED.trigger: Required cross-validation is absent, incomplete, or failed.
ERR_CROSS_VALIDATION_BLOCKED.remedy: Run all declared checks and integrate only after they pass.

stale_revision_behavior.on_mismatch: Reject before target execution with ERR_HANDOFF_STALE_REVISION.
forbidden: Replaying the target on a newer revision
Copying only selected fields into a fresh envelope
recovery: Re-resolve accepted decisions, evidence, maturity, bottleneck, dependencies, and tactic eligibility.
Regenerate the complete handoff with a new handoff_id and current revision, or append an explicit revalidation record linked to the old handoff.
Rerun scope, provenance, dependency, tactic, and parallel-safety validation before the target starts.
stale_revision_behavior.integration: All specialist outputs remain staged. Loop Planning performs one compare-and-swap integration against the shared input revision; a failed compare-and-swap restarts validation.

evidence_and_claim_rules.fact: Every factual claim resolves through evidence_refs to source_ref and observed_at. Repetition by a user or agent does not make a hypothesis a fact.
evidence_and_claim_rules.unsupported_claim: An unsupported interpretation is serialized only as a hypothesis represented by exactly one assumptions item with claim_refs, rationale, and confidence. It cannot advance state, maturity, score, bottleneck, or an accepted decision.
evidence_and_claim_rules.empty_evidence_refs: Allowed only when assumptions is non-empty and semantic validation proves that every claim in the handoff is unsupported and mapped to an assumption. The target must remain in evidence-seeking behavior.
evidence_and_claim_rules.decisions: Each respected decision retains owner, revision, summary, and at least one evidence reference. The target consumes it without silent redefinition.
evidence_and_claim_rules.reference_only: Inputs and tactics are referenced, not copied. Raw customer corpora, secrets, credentials, and unnecessary PII never enter the envelope.
evidence_and_claim_rules.external_execution: Evidence of planning or recommendation is not authorization evidence. External execution remains blocked until an authorized operator supplies operation-specific authority.

maturity_and_tactic_rules.freshness: Reuse an evidenced maturity classification only when at most 30 days old and no later structural-change event exists; otherwise recompute.
maturity_and_tactic_rules.unknown: Unknown is not nascente. It forces minimo_viavel, zero tactic_refs, specialist base method, cross-validation, and escalation to loop_planning.

cardinality.zero: Run the specialist base method when it is sufficient, no exact eligible match exists, metadata is incomplete, or every match is gated.
cardinality.one: Default when one eligible tactic covers the requested tactical output.
cardinality.two: Allowed only for two distinct required outputs or a declared dependency, with both tactics eligible and noncolliding.
cardinality.more_than_two: Reject the route node with ERR_TACTIC_CARDINALITY.

maturity_and_tactic_rules.identity: Each tactic_ref contains exactly tactic_id, canonical_path, canonical_sha256, and selection_reason and must resolve byte-for-byte through P3 metadata/source index.
maturity_and_tactic_rules.target_binding: A selected tactic belongs to the target specialist pillar. Loop Planning can select and reference it but does not inherit its domain authority.

parallel_preconditions: All nodes read the same immutable state_revision.
No node requires another parallel node's new output.
All required producer handoffs were validated before the wave.
Loop Planning is the sole integrator.
concurrency_rules.staging: Specialists emit immutable staged outputs and proposed events; they never write accepted shared state directly.
concurrency_rules.collision: A shared write path yields ERR_PARALLEL_WRITE_COLLISION. No timestamp, completion order, confidence, or last-writer-wins rule resolves it.
concurrency_rules.stale_member: A stale handoff cannot replay in the wave. Revalidate or serialize the affected nodes before integration.
concurrency_rules.cross_validation: Every multi-output cycle requires cross-validation before the one compare-and-swap integration.

positive.name: Loop Planning to Verbalizar with one eligible tactic and an accepted audience decision

instance.objective: Definir a hierarquia de mensagem para o segmento enterprise no estágio de consideração.

tactic_refs.selection_reason: Uma hierarquia de mensagem é o output tático específico solicitado e a proposta de valor aceita já está disponível.

decisions_to_respect.summary: O segmento enterprise e o estágio de consideração já foram aceitos e não podem ser redefinidos por Verbalizar.

known_gaps.description: Há prova quantitativa parcial para uma mensagem de suporte.

requested_output.description: Publicar proposta de hierarquia de mensagem, provas, objeções e CTA sem redefinir audiência, canal ou teste.
acceptance_criteria: Mantém o segmento e estágio aceitos.
Não escolhe canal, cadência ou métrica experimental.

escalation_conditions.when: A mensagem exigir alteração do segmento ou estágio aceito.
escalation_conditions.action: Emitir proposta fundamentada ao owner sem redefinir a decisão.

positive.name: Unknown maturity falls back to minimum-viable Refinar base method with assumptions only

instance.objective: Auditar os dados disponíveis e produzir um plano mínimo de coleta sem diagnosticar desempenho sem evidência.

assumptions.statement: Os dados de desempenho parecem incompletos para diagnóstico.
assumptions.rationale: O pedido informa ausência de série comparável, mas nenhuma fonte foi fornecida.

known_gaps.description: Faltam períodos comparáveis, definição de métrica e proveniência.

requested_output.description: Produzir inventário de lacunas e requisitos de coleta, sem emitir diagnóstico ou causalidade.
acceptance_criteria: Toda ausência é registrada como gap.
Nenhuma hipótese é tratada como fato.
Nenhum experimento avança além de proposed.

escalation_conditions.when: O diagnóstico exigir fatos ou séries ainda ausentes.
escalation_conditions.action: Solicitar dados e manter o route status em needs_evidence.

escalation_conditions.when: A seleção de tática ou método acima do mínimo for solicitada.
escalation_conditions.action: Reclassificar maturidade com evidência antes de selecionar tática.

negative.mutation: Set validation_context.current_state_revision=8 while handoff.state_revision and target_read_revision remain 7.
Add channel_timing_and_cadence to requested_output.decision_domains for to_role=verbalizar.
Remove experiment_performance_and_learning from the scope boundary.
Set assumptions=[] while evidence_refs remains empty.
Add one tactic_ref while maturity remains unknown.
Duplicate valid tactic_ref twice more so tactic_refs has three items.
Change the required decision input dependency_status to not_applicable without an explicit route decision.
Place a second staged handoff at revision 8 in the same parallel batch.
Place a second staged handoff whose write_set contains state.proposals.message-contract.
Change objective to instruct direct publication to a live external channel without an authorization gap or authorized_operator escalation.

ambiguity_log.p2_ambiguity: P2 requires opaque references but does not define a wire-format pattern.
ambiguity_log.proposal: Use namespaced non-secret strings such as project:, cycle:, evidence:, decision:, and artifact: with bounded length.
ambiguity_log.safe_default: Reject unnamespaced, whitespace-bearing, secret-bearing, or raw-corpus values.

ambiguity_log.p2_ambiguity: The handoff field requires a reference to the accepted bottleneck, while a needs_evidence route can legitimately have no accepted bottleneck.
ambiguity_log.proposal: Require an explicit loop_planning unresolved-bottleneck record, never null and never a specialist observation.
ambiguity_log.safe_default: If neither accepted nor explicit unresolved record exists, reject with ERR_HANDOFF_PROVENANCE.

ambiguity_log.p2_ambiguity: P2 says a boolean accompanied by roles or conflicts but does not define the nested object.
ambiguity_log.proposal: Serialize exactly required, roles, and conflicts; require at least one role or conflict when true and empty arrays when false.
ambiguity_log.safe_default: Ambiguous cross-validation intent is required=true and escalates to loop_planning.

ambiguity_log.p2_ambiguity: P2 defines rejection codes but not a primary-code order when one handoff violates several rules.
ambiguity_log.proposal: Return all violations in deterministic rule order and expose the first mapped code as primary_code.
ambiguity_log.safe_default: Never suppress a later authority, provenance, or authorization violation because an earlier structural error exists.

ambiguity_log.p2_ambiguity: P2 does not explicitly state whether from_role may equal to_role.
ambiguity_log.proposal: Reject same-role envelopes as ERR_HANDOFF_OWNER_SCOPE; represent internal continuation as a route node or append-only event instead.

ambiguity_log.p2_ambiguity: Roles escalate to authorized_operator, but authorized_operator is not a canonical role object and to_role must exist in the role matrix.
ambiguity_log.proposal: Keep authorized_operator outside from_role/to_role and use it only as gap owner or escalation target until P6 defines an execution contract.
ambiguity_log.safe_default: Analytical handoff remains read-only and cannot imply external authorization.

verdict.release_note: This is a P4 schema and validator proposal. It does not authorize external execution, alter P2/P3 artifacts, or modify canonical prompts.
